text:
There once was a quiet village in the Alps. It's a place where sheep outnumbered people, and the silence between the meadows was only broken by the distant sound of bells. In this village lived an old woman named Helena. She had lived there all her life, knitting intricate hats for the sheep.

One snow-laden Christmas Eve, Helena found her knitting gone. Fearing harm to her precious woolen creations, she decided to search outside her cozy cottage. On her chilly hunt, Helena stumbled across a mysterious old chapel concealed behind a thicket. Intrigued, she pushed through the brush.

Inside, she found something she'd never dreamt to see: not her missing knitting, but a collection of beautiful, intricately woven masks. Each mask was unlike any she'd ever seen—a reflection of secrets, faces hiding stories.

Curious, Helena picked up one mask. As she stared into its eyes, it felt like she looked into hers. There was a silence between them.
